adman: noun. A person in the business of devising, writing, illustrating or selling advertisements.
aga: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Aguano.
again: adverb. Another time: indicating a repeat of an action.
aid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Alngith.
aidman: noun. An army medical corpsman attached to a field unit.
aim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Aimol.
ain: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Ainu.
amin: noun. An arbitrator who assessed and collected revenue in the pargana.
ana: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andaqui.
and: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ansus.
ani: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andi.
anima: noun. The soul or animating principle of a living thing, especially as contrasted with the animus.
dag: symbol. Symbol for decagram, an SI unit of mass equal to 10 grams.
dagga: noun. Indian hemp, Cannabis sativa subsp. indica, or a similar plant of the species Leonotis leonurus.
dam: symbol. Symbol for decameter, an SI unit of length equal to 10 meters.
ding: noun. Very minor damage caused by being struck; a small dent or chip.
gad: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gaddang.
gadi: noun. A cushioned throne used by Indian princes.
gag: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gagauz.
gagman: noun. A writer or performer of gags.
gan: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gan.
gid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gidar.
mad: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Madurese.
mag: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Magahi.
maid: noun. A girl or an unmarried young woman; maiden.
main: adjective. Of chief or leading importance; prime, principal.
man: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mandingo.
mana: noun. Power, prestige; specifically, a form of supernatural energy in Polynesian religion that inheres in things or people.
mania: noun. Violent derangement of mind; madness; insanity.
mind: noun. The capability for rational thought.
nag: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Naga Pidgin.
naiad: noun. A female deity (nymph) associated with water, especially a spring, stream, or other fresh water.
nam: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ngan'gityemerri.
nim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nilamba.
